title: Tangy Molten British Chicken Fried Rice
description: A quick and tangy British dish featuring chicken and whole milk, with a crispy double-fried coating, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat 1 cup of vegetable oil in a deep frying pan to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, and pepper.
Dip each piece of chicken into the flour mixture, then into the whole milk, and finally into the flour mixture again.
Double-fry the chicken by frying it for 5 minutes, then letting it rest for 5 minutes, and finally frying it for an additional 5 minutes.
In a separate pan, scramble the eggs and set them aside.
Add the cooked rice to the same pan and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the scrambled eggs, tangy sauce, and cooked chicken to the rice and stir-fry for an additional 2 minutes.
Serve the Tangy Molten British Chicken Fried Rice hot and enjoy.
Cook poultry to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F before serving.
